items.title: Rust-proof your code with our new Testing Handbook chapter
items.summary: We’ve added a new chapter to our Testing Handbook: a comprehensive guide to security testing Rust programs. This chapter covers the tools and techniques we use at Trail of Bits to validate the security of Rust programs and systems. fn main() {(|f:&dyn Fn(u128)->Box< dyn Iterator<

items.title: GPT-5.5-Cyber built a zlib fuzzing lab in a day
items.summary: We’re running Patch the Planet, an ongoing collaboration with OpenAI that pairs Trail of Bits engineers directly with more than 30 open-source projects. Its goal is to front-run a serious problem facing open-source maintainers: highly capable models like GPT-5.5-Cyber will soon c

items.title: Bitcoin wilts as oil and rates rise. Clarity Act odds tumble to 38%
items.summary: Geopolitical risks, rising rates, and fresh regulatory setbacks send crypto lower as key Democrats demand stronger safeguards in the market structure bill.

items.title: Bitcoin retreats from one-month high as oil tops $85, inflation concerns resurface
items.summary: BTC retreated from a one-month high as WTI crude topped $85 for the first time since June, reigniting inflation concerns and pushing investors toward gold, silver and the safety of bitcoin over altcoins.

items.title: Mutation testing comes to DAML
items.summary: In April we released Mewt, our open-source mutation-testing engine that finds the gaps in your test suite. Today we’re expanding it with support for DAML, the language Canton Network applications are written in. Mewt now reads DAML, generates several classes of mutants (including

items.title: Live markets: Bitcoin trades above $65,000 as Alphabet's bigger AI bill props up the chip trade
items.summary: Alphabet beat on revenue but raised its AI spending forecast again, sending its stock lower after hours. For the chip complex bitcoin tracks, more spending is the bullish read.

items.title: BitMEX, the exchange that invented perps, is shutting down
items.summary: BitMEX, co-founded by Arthur Hayes in 2014, redefined the global crypto market by introducing the 100x leverage perpetual swap.

items.title: 24/7 financial rails: How BNY plans to eliminate the weekend lag in U.S. Treasuries
items.summary: The bank will begin testing tokenized Treasuries on a private blockchain by year-end and expand its settlement network to cover more international trading hours.

items.title: South Korea's oldest crypto exchange is now part of the $1 trillion Mirae Group family
items.summary: Korbit, South Korea's first cryptocurrency exchange, has joined the Mirae Asset Group family, marking the first time a traditional Korean financial group's affiliate has taken a controlling stake in a domestic crypto exchange.

items.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum-linked protocols lose $35 million in multiple attacks hours apart
items.summary: The attacks hit Verus, B² Network and other cross-chain systems, showing how compromised keys, upgrade powers and validation checks can empty protocols without breaking the underlying cryptography.

items.title: Arbitrum-based AFX Trade drained of $24 million after bridge keys compromised
items.summary: Security firms said the attacker used enough hot-validator signatures to approve a 24.15 million USDC withdrawal, while Arbitrum said its native bridge was not affected.

items.title: XRP whales accumulate as small holders capitulate
items.summary: XRP whales accumulate 2.8% more tokens over five weeks as small holders capitulate, sending the price back above $1.16.

items.title: Bitcoin may have bottomed before its traditional cycle low: Grayscale’s Pandl
items.summary: Grayscale’s head of research said that Bitcoin may have bottomed, arguing that macro factors, such as interest rate decisions as increasingly driving the price action of this maturing asset class.

items.title: Home invasions became most common crypto wrench attack in H1 2026: CertiK
items.summary: Crypto home invasions rose to 20 in H1 2026 from one a year earlier, while France accounted for 33 of 52 verified wrench attacks, according to CertiK.

items.title: BitMEX to shut down after 11 years in crypto derivatives
items.summary: BitMEX announced it will shut down operations on Sept. 23, 2026, after owner HDR Global Trading Limited decided to close the crypto exchange.

items.title: Mirae Asset completes Korbit acquisition, becomes largest shareholder
items.summary: Mirae Asset Consulting has become Korbit’s largest shareholder after completing its acquisition, with the exchange saying trading and customer asset protections are unchanged.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs approach $1B in 7-session inflow run
items.summary: US spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $69 million in inflows on Wednesday, extending their inflow streak to seven sessions and bringing total inflows during the period to nearly $1 billion

items.title: Kakao taps Circle to explore won stablecoin payment infrastructure
items.summary: Kakao Group and Circle signed an MOU to explore won stablecoin payments, remittances, merchant settlement and tokenized financial services.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Swiss bank BancaStato launches regulated crypto trading with Sygnum
items.summary: Swiss cantonal bank BancaStato integrated Sygnum’s crypto trading and custody services into its Avaloq-powered digital banking apps for Bitcoin and other assets.
